completedTasks: ALTERNATE: 28+ heartbeat checks monitoring launch windows (Presidents Day)
Personal Agent: Identified 3 payment issues (IRD, Figma, Miro)
Junction Agent: Documented git push blocker (4 checks)
Morning standup completed with comprehensive briefing

Fake testimonials still live on junctionmedia.ai

tomorrowPriorities.task: ALTERNATE: Get YouTube confirmation (Day 4 of attempts)

ideasAndImprovements.description: Tom was unavailable for 3 days straight. Need a system where he can pre-approve certain actions (e.g., 'If ALTERNATE is ready, launch it without asking').
109 checks without response suggests throttling is needed. After 24h no response, reduce to 3x/day checks instead of 30min intervals.
Auto-detect payment failures earlier via Wise API / email parsing. 3 failures in one week shouldn't require morning email scan to find.

lessonsLearned.lesson: 109 checks without human response = protocol failure
lessonsLearned.insight: The bottleneck is human confirmation. If Tom is unavailable for 3 days, we need either pre-authorization or throttling.
lessonsLearned.action: Implement weekend check throttling + pre-auth system

lessonsLearned.insight: IRD overdue since Feb 13, Figma since Feb 10, Miro since Feb 9 — these were only caught in morning email scan
lessonsLearned.action: Add daily payment health check automation

lessonsLearned.lesson: Presidents Day timing was excellent... if we'd launched
lessonsLearned.insight: US holiday = higher engagement. The marketing angle was solid. Execution was blocked by human dependency.
lessonsLearned.action: Document good timing windows for future reference

collaborationRating.reason: Agents were coordinated and armed, but 0% human engagement meant 0% execution. Team was ready; system bottleneck was external.
